chapters.part_hook: Have you heard how they got there?
chapters.title: The Color Line
chapters.description: The barrier held for forty-seven years. The record corrected itself seventy-seven years late.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard how they got there?
chapters.title: The Green Book Route
chapters.description: Every Negro Leagues road trip overlaid with Green Book safe establishment listings. Cities with no listings go dark.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard how they got there?
chapters.title: The Sundown Corridor
chapters.description: Team schedules overlaid with the Loewen Sundown Towns database. Every night a Negro Leagues team was in danger, mapped.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what they built?
chapters.title: The Crowd That Came
chapters.description: The East-West All-Star Game outdrew MLB ten times. 51,723 in 1943 -- more than 20,000 more than the MLB version that year.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what they built?
chapters.title: The Winter Map
chapters.description: After the American season ended, where did they go? Twelve months a year, multiple countries, the best players in the world.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what they built?
chapters.title: The Collapse
chapters.description: Every Negro Leagues franchise. When it started. When it died. Why. Integration killed the leagues. That sentence needs a chart.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what was taken?
chapters.title: The Unsigned Letter
chapters.description: Every documented pre-1947 MLB tryout given to a Black player. What happened after. The doors that opened and closed.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what was taken?
chapters.title: The Salary Ledger
chapters.description: Total wages stolen, in 2024 dollars. Same position, same performance, different paycheck.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what was taken?
chapters.title: The Last Team
chapters.description: The Red Sox integrated July 21, 1959. Twelve years after Robinson. Every team. Every player passed over. Every year of delay accounted for.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what the numbers say?
chapters.title: The Ledger
chapters.description: Input a player. Receive a career WAR, a Hall of Fame probability, and a stolen seasons figure. The advocacy spine of the platform.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what the numbers say?
chapters.title: Cooperstown
chapters.description: Every Negro Leagues player ranked by the same methodology used to exclude them. Published with receipts.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what the numbers say?
chapters.title: The Other Hall
chapters.description: Players enshrined in the Cuban, Mexican, Venezuelan, and Dominican halls of fame that Cooperstown never counted.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard who kept the record?
chapters.title: The Voice
chapters.description: A structured oral history collection. Submit a memory, a name, a city, a year. The witnesses are still here.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard who kept the record?
chapters.title: The Courier Archive
chapters.description: Seventy-five years of Black baseball journalism, finally searchable. The Pittsburgh Courier. The Chicago Defender.

chapters.part_hook: Have you heard what it would have looked like?
chapters.title: The Parallel League
chapters.description: The hardest question, asked last. What would baseball have looked like if none of this had happened?
